description: User scrolls through the initial Google search results for 'best places to go london'.
page_title: best places to go london - Google Search
frame_description: Google search results page showing various travel articles about London.

description: User clicks into the search bar to modify their search query.
page_title: best places to go london - Google Search
frame_description: The search bar is focused and the cursor is positioned inside the text area.

description: Google search autocomplete suggestions dropdown appears as the user interacts with the search bar.
frame_description: A list of suggested search terms appears below the search input field.

description: User types 'stay in london' into the search bar to refine their search.
frame_description: The text in the search bar updates to 'best places to stay in london'.

description: The page navigates to a new set of search results after the user submits the refined query.
frame_description: The screen refreshes and displays new search results focused on accommodation in London.

description: User scrolls down the new search results page to browse accommodation guides.
page_title: best places to stay in london - Google Search
frame_description: The user is moving down the page, passing sponsored results and organic links.

description: The cursor hovers over a search result link from 'Destination Well Known'.
interaction_target: Where to Stay in London | Best Areas & Places to Stay
frame_description: The cursor pauses over the blue link of the Destination Well Known search result.

description: User continues scrolling down the search results.
frame_description: The page content moves upwards as the user scrolls.

description: User scrolls back up the page, likely re-evaluating previous results.
frame_description: The page content moves downwards as the user scrolls up.

description: Cursor hovers over a search result by Janice Rothstein.
interaction_target: Where to Stay in London: The Best Neighborhoods & Hotels
frame_description: The cursor is stationary over the Janice Rothstein search result link.

description: User scrolls down the page again.
frame_description: The user is browsing further down the list of search results.

description: User scrolls back up the page.
frame_description: The user returns to the middle section of the search results page.

description: Cursor hovers over a search result from 'A Lady in London'.
interaction_target: 15 Places to Stay in London Based on Your Personal Style
frame_description: The cursor lingers over the 'A Lady in London' search result.
The user continues to explore the search results.

description: User scrolls back up the page.
frame_description: The user moves back up to a previous section of the results.
The user scrolls towards the bottom of the first page of results.

description: User clicks on a different browser tab to switch to the Hostelworld website.
frame_description: The cursor is positioned over the Hostelworld tab in the browser's tab bar.

description: The browser switches to the Hostelworld tab, displaying a map and list of hostels.
frame_description: The screen changes to show the Hostelworld interface with a map of London and hostel listings.
